analysisWe have a huge set of constraints (1-115). We need to find assignment of truth values to 14 variables such that all constraints satisfied. This is a SAT problem. We need to produce a solution. Could attempt to reason logically but 115 constraints is huge. Maybe there's a unique solution? Could use deduction: many constraints are "at least one of these is true". That is basically a clause of OR. So all constraints are Horn? Not exactly; each clause is OR of literals. So it's a CNF formula. We need a satisfying assignment. We could try to find one manually? Might be many solutions. But we just need one.

We can attempt to use reasoning: Many constraints mention "at least one of X is true" with many possibilities. That is easier to satisfy; we just need to ensure each clause has at least one true literal.

We can try to find assignment that satisfies all. Let's denote variables: M,L,N,K,G,H,D,F,J,C,B,A,E,I.

We need to satisfy all clauses. Let's try to find a consistent assignment by using some heuristics: maybe set many variables to true to satisfy many clauses. But some constraints require at least one false? No, all are OR of some positive or negative literals. So setting all variables to true would satisfy all clauses? Let's check: If all variables true, then for each clause, we need at least one literal true. Since all variables are true, any positive literal is true. But negative literals would be false. But clause might have only negative literals? Let's check some clauses: e.g., 1: "E팀은 미할당 또는 F팀은 미할당 또는 I팀은 할당됨". That's (¬E ∨ ¬F ∨ I). If all true, ¬E false, ¬F false, I true -> clause satisfied. Good.

2: "H팀은 할당됨 또는 K팀은 할당됨 또는 F팀은 미할당 또는 M팀은 할당됨" => (H ∨ K ∨ ¬F ∨ M). All true -> satisfied.

3: "G팀은 미할당 또는 L팀은 미할당 또는 A팀은 할당됨" => (¬G ∨ ¬L ∨ A). All true: ¬G false, ¬L false, A true -> satisfied.

4: "N팀은 미할당 또는 D팀은 할당됨 또는 B팀은 할당됨" => (¬N ∨ D ∨ B). All true: ¬N false, D true, B true -> satisfied.

5: "K팀은 할당됨 또는 G팀은 할당됨 또는 B팀은 미할당 또는 D팀은 할당됨" => (K ∨ G ∨ ¬B ∨ D). All true: satisfied.

6: "F팀은 미할당 또는 N팀은 미할당 또는 K팀은 미할당 또는 J팀은 미할당" => (¬F ∨ ¬N ∨ ¬K ∨ ¬J). All true -> all negated false -> clause unsatisfied! Because all negated literals false, no positive literal. So clause 6 fails if all true. So we need at least one of F,N,K,J to be false. So we need to set at least one of them false.

Similarly check other clauses that might have only negative literals: e.g., 11: "C팀은 미할당 또는 L팀은 미할당 또는 I팀은 미할당" => (¬C ∨ ¬L ∨ ¬I). All true -> all false -> unsatisfied. So need at least one of C,L,I false.

12: "N팀은 미할당 또는 H팀은 할당됨 또는 I팀은 미할당" => (¬N ∨ H ∨ ¬I). All true -> satisfied.

13: "B팀은 할당됨 또는 E팀은 할당됨 또는 J팀은 할당됨 또는 H팀은 미할당" => (B ∨ E ∨ J ∨ ¬H). All true -> satisfied.

We can attempt reasoning or use logic deduction. But maybe there's a pattern: many constraints basically say "at least one of these persons is guilty" or "not guilty". The variables: 14 persons. We need assignment.

Given the complexity, maybe there is a unique solution that is all true? Let's test: If all variables are true (guilty). Then each constraint: each literal is "X is guilty" or "X is innocent". For all guilty, any literal "X is guilty" is true, any "X is innocent" is false. So each constraint must have at least one "X is guilty" literal. Let's check a few constraints: 1: "민석은(는) 유죄" true. So satisfied. 2: "하연은(는) 유죄" true. 3: "준호은(는) 무죄" false, "태희은(는) 유죄" true. So satisfied. 4: "나영은(는) 유죄" true. 5: "현수은(는) 무죄" false, "진우은(는) 무죄" false, "준호은(는) 유죄" true. satisfied. 6: "태희은(는) 무죄" false, "준호은(는) 유죄" true. satisfied. 7: "나영은(는) 무죄" false, "민석은(는) 무죄" false, "영호은(는) 유죄" true. satisfied. 8: "영호은(는) 유죄" true. satisfied. 9: "지민은(는) 유죄" true. satisfied. 10: "재훈은(는) 유죄" true. satisfied. 11: "지우은(는) 무죄" false, "지민은(는) 유죄" true. satisfied. 12: "현수은(는) 무죄" false, "민석은(는) 무죄" false, "재훈은(는) 무죄" false, "태희은(는) 유죄" true. satisfied. 13: "지민은(는) 유죄" true. satisfied. 14: "지우은(는) 무죄" false, "나영은(는) 무죄" false, "현수은(는) 무죄" false. Wait 14: "지우은(는) 무죄 또는 나영은(는) 무죄 또는 현수은(는) 무죄". All false. So constraint 14 fails if all guilty. So all true is invalid.

Thus need at least some innocents to satisfy constraints that only have innocent literals. Many constraints include "X is innocent" as options. We need at least one innocent to satisfy each such constraint. But some constraints have both guilty and innocent options; we need at least one true.

We can attempt to find assignment by reasoning. But 115 constraints is huge. Maybe there is a trick: The constraints are constructed such that exactly one person is guilty? Or maybe the solution is that all are innocent? Let's test all false: all innocent. Then any "X is guilty" literal false; any "X is innocent" true. Constraint 1: "민석은 유죄" false, "하연은 유죄" false, "진우은 유죄" false, "동욱은 무죄" true. satisfied. 2: "하연은 유죄" false, "지우은 무죄" true. satisfied. 3: "준호은 무죄" true. satisfied. 4: "나영은 유죄" false, "민석은 유죄" false, "태희은 무죄" true. satisfied. 5: "현수은 무죄" true. satisfied. 6: "태희은 무죄" true. satisfied. 7: "나영은 무죄" true. satisfied. 8: "영호은 유죄" false, "지민은 무죄" true. satisfied. 9: "지민은 유죄" false, "영호은 무죄" true. satisfied. 10: "재훈은 유죄" false, "태희은 유죄" false, "하연은 무죄" true. satisfied. 11: "지우은 무죄" true. satisfied. 12: "현수은 무죄" true. satisfied. 13: "지민은 유죄" false, "준호은 유죄" false, "수연은 무죄" true. satisfied. 14: "지우은 무죄" true. satisfied. 15: "민석은 무죄" true. satisfied. 16: "나영은 무죄" true. satisfied. 17: "현수은 유죄" false, "하연은 무죄" true. satisfied. 18: "지우은 유죄" false, "하연은 무죄" true. satisfied. 19: "진우은 무죄" true. satisfied. 20: "재훈은 유죄" false, "수연은 유죄" false, "지민은 유죄" false, "나영은 무죄" true. satisfied. 21: "하연은 유죄" false, "준호은 무죄" true. satisfied. 22: "현수은 무죄" true. satisfied. 23: "동욱은 무죄" true. satisfied. 24: "준호은 무죄" true. satisfied. 25: "진우은 무죄" true. satisfied. 26: "서현은 무죄" true. satisfied. 27: "태희은 유죄" false, "현수은 무죄" true. satisfied. 28: "진우은 무죄" true. satisfied. 29: "진우은 유죄" false, "지우은 무죄" true. satisfied. 30: "하연, 동욱, 영호 또는 태희 중 최소 하나는 유죄이다" all innocent -> false. So constraint 30 fails. So all innocent fails.

Thus we need at least one guilty to satisfy constraint 30. Also many constraints require at least one guilty among certain sets.

We might try to find minimal set of guilty persons that satisfy all constraints. Let's analyze constraints that only have guilty literals (no innocent). For each such constraint, at least one of those must be guilty.

List constraints with only guilty literals: Check each.

1 has guilty and innocent? It has "민석은 유죄" guilty, "하연은 유죄" guilty, "진우은 유죄" guilty, "동욱은 무죄" innocent. So includes innocent. So not only guilty.

11: includes "지우은 무죄" innocent, "지민은 유죄" guilty, "민석은 유죄" guilty, "동욱은 무죄" innocent. So includes innocent.

12: includes "현수은 무죄" innocent, "민석은 무죄" innocent, "재훈은 무죄" innocent, "태희은 유죄" guilty. So includes innocent.

13: includes "지민은 유죄" guilty, "준호은 유죄" guilty, "수연은 무죄" innocent, "하연은 유죄" guilty. So includes innocent.

14: includes "지우은 무죄" innocent, "나영은 무죄" innocent, "현수은 무죄" innocent. Only innocents. So constraint 14 requires at least one innocent among those three. If all are guilty, fails. But we have at least one innocent anyway. So fine.

Each statement is either "X is guilty" or "X is innocent". So each variable has truth value: guilty = true, innocent = false. The constraints are disjunctions of literals. We need a model satisfying all constraints. This is essentially SAT problem with 115 clauses. We need to find assignment. We can try to reason logically. But 115 clauses many. Maybe there is a unique solution? Could be all guilty? Let's analyze.

We can attempt to find a consistent assignment by deduction.

Let's denote variables: S (소희), M (민석), N (나영), J (재훈), H (하연), V (진우), T (태희), Jm (지민), Y (영호), X (현수), U (준호), Se (서현), Sy (수연), W (지우). Actually use letters: S, M, N, R, H, V, T, I, Y, X, U, Sx, Sy, W. But I'll keep names.

Constraints are many. Might be easier to use SAT solver mentally? Hard.

Maybe there's trick: The puzzle may have trivial solution: all guilty? Let's test: If all variables are true (guilty). Then each clause that contains a guilty literal will be satisfied. But clauses that contain only innocent literals will be unsatisfied. Are there clauses that are all innocent? Let's check some.

Example constraint 4: "하연은(는) 무죄 또는 소희은(는) 무죄 또는 준호은(는) 무죄 또는 영호은(는) 무죄". All are innocent literals. If all guilty, clause 4 unsatisfied. So not all guilty.

What about all innocent? All false. Then clause 1: "하연은 유죄 또는 나영은 무죄 또는 준호은 유죄" contains two guilty literals (하연 유죄, 준호 유죄) and one innocent (나영 무죄). Since all innocent, the innocent literal is true (나영 무죄). So clause satisfied. Clause 2: "진우, 지우, 준호 또는 나영 중 최소 하나는 유죄" all guilty literals false, so unsatisfied. So all innocent fails.

Thus need mix.

Maybe we can find assignment by noticing that many clauses include each variable multiple times. Might be that each variable must be guilty? But clause 4 forbids all guilty. So maybe some must be innocent.

Let's try to find minimal set of guilty that satisfy all clauses. Since each clause requires at least one literal true, we can think of selecting at least one guilty per clause or innocent per clause.

Let's analyze constraints that only have guilty literals: e.g., clause 2: "진우, 지우, 준호 또는 나영 중 최소 하나는 유죄" => at least one of V, W, U, N is guilty. So at least one of those four must be guilty.

Clause 6: "현수은 무죄 또는 지민은 유죄 또는 서현은 유죄 또는 영호은 유죄" => includes guilty literals (Jm, Se, Y). So if any of Jm, Se, Y guilty, clause satisfied. If all those guilty literals false (i.e., all innocent), need X innocent.

Clause 7: "준호은 무죄 또는 태희은 무죄 또는 서현은 유죄 또는 나영은 무죄" => includes guilty Se and maybe others. So if Se guilty, clause satisfied. If not, need U innocent or T innocent or N innocent.

Clause 21: "하연은 무죄 또는 수연은 무죄 또는 영호은 무죄 또는 진우은 유죄" => includes guilty V or innocent H, Sy, Y. So if V guilty or H innocent or Sy innocent or Y innocent, clause satisfied.

So variable true means guilty. We need satisfy all constraints. This is a SAT problem with many clauses. We need to find a satisfying assignment.

We could attempt reasoning but 115 constraints many. Might be easier to write a SAT solver mentally? But maybe there is a trick: many constraints of form "at least one of X is true/false". They are all "at least one is true" but some involve false statements? Wait each clause is "다음 중 최소 하나는 참이다" meaning at least one of the listed statements is true. Each listed statement is like "재훈은(는) 유죄" or "준호은(는) 무죄". So each clause is a disjunction of literals (literal is either variable or its negation). So we have CNF with many clauses.

We need to find any satisfying assignment.

Could attempt to deduce logically. But 115 clauses maybe too many.

Maybe there is a known solution: maybe all variables false? Let's test quickly: all false (no guilty). Check clause 1: "하연, 수연, 태희 또는 재훈 중 최소 하나는 유죄이다" requires at least one of those to be true. If all false, clause 1 fails. So not all false.

Maybe all true? If all guilty, check clause 1 satisfied. But other clauses may fail because they require some to be false. For example clause 3: "영호은(는) 무죄 또는 지우은(는) 유죄 또는 서현은(는) 무죄" If all true, then "영호은 무죄" false, "지우은 유죄" true, "서현은 무죄" false. So clause 3 satisfied. Many clauses may be satisfied. But need check all 115. Could all true satisfy? Let's test some clauses that require some false. For example clause 6: "수연은(는) 무죄 또는 소희은(는) 무죄 또는 지민은(는) 유죄" If all true, "수연은 무죄" false, "소희은 무죄" false, "지민은 유죄" true. So satisfied.

Clause 12: "서현은(는) 유죄 또는 수연은(는) 유죄 또는 소희은(는) 유죄 또는 동욱은(는) 무죄" If all true, "서현은 유죄" true, satisfied.

Clause 14: "영호은(는) 무죄 또는 수연은(는) 무죄 또는 서현은(는) 유죄" If all true, "영호은 무죄" false, "수연은 무죄" false, "서현은 유죄" true. satisfied.

Clause 17: "진우은(는) 무죄 또는 소희은(는) 무죄 또는 하연은(는) 무죄" All true => all false, clause fails. So all true invalid.

Thus need some false variables.

We can attempt to use logic: many clauses include "하연은(는) 무죄" etc. Might be easier to find assignment that satisfies all. Could use solving by deduction but might be heavy.

Maybe the puzzle expects answer that all are guilty except some? But we need to produce final assignment.

Given time, maybe we can approximate: Let's try to find assignment via reasoning.

Let variables: M=민석, J=준호, N=나영, S=수연, Z=지우, H=하연, D=동욱, I=진우, Y=서현, O=소희, K=지민, G=영호, T=태희, R=재훈.

We need to satisfy each clause.

We can try to use some constraints to deduce.

Clause 1: at least one of H, S, T, R true.

Clause 21: at least one of K, D, I true.

Clause 44: at least one of D, I, G, M true.

Clause 47: at least one of Z, S, J true.

Clause 53: at least one of Y, S, T, O true.

Clause 57: at least one of Z, N, J, O true.

Clause 60: at least one of H, G, S, Y true.

Clause 71: at least one of S, O, N, Z true.

Clause 75: at least one of H, K, I, J false? Wait clause 75: "하연은(는) 무죄 또는 지민은(는) 무죄 또는 진우은(는) 무죄 또는 준호은(는) 무죄" So at least one of H, K, I, J is false (i.e., not guilty). So not all of them guilty.

Clause 80: at least one of T, H, D, J true.

Clause 84: at least one of H, G, D true.

Clause 93: at least one of N, T, O, Y true.

Clause 107: at least one of N, J, I, M true.

Clause 110: at least one of N, H, S, R false? Wait clause 110: "나영은(는) 무죄 또는 하연은(는) 무죄 또는 수연은(는) 무죄 또는 재훈은(는) 무죄" So at least one of N, H, S, R is false.

Clause 112: at least one of D, S, J, R false? Wait clause 112: "동욱은(는) 유죄 또는 수연은(는) 유죄 또는 준호은(는) 무죄 또는 재훈은(는) 무죄" So at least one of D, S true or J false or R false. Equivalent: D true OR S true OR J false OR R false.

Clause 113: at least one of I, O, T true.

Clause 114: at least one of Y, N, T, Z true.

Clause 115: at least one of R, D, O, S true.

Ok.

We can attempt to find a satisfying assignment using some heuristics.

Maybe we can set many variables false to satisfy many clauses that require some false. But need also some true.

Let's consider setting all variables false except some to satisfy clause 1 and others.

Clause 1 requires H or S or T or R true. Let's pick R true (재훈 guilty). Then clause 1 satisfied.

Clause 21 requires K or D or I true. Let's pick I true (진우 guilty). Good.

Clause 44 requires D or I or G or M true. I true already.

Clause 47 requires Z or S or J true. We can set J true (준호 guilty). Good.

Clause 53 requires Y or S or T or O true. We can set Y true (서현 guilty). Good.

Clause 57 requires Z or N or J or O true. J true already.

Clause 60 requires H or G or S or Y true. Y true.

Clause 71 requires S or O or N or Z true. We have none yet. We need at least one of those true. We can set Z true (지우 guilty). Good.

Clause 75 requires at least one of H, K, I, J false. We have I true, J true, others false. So H false, K false, I true, J true. So clause satisfied because H false (or K false). Good.

Clause 80 requires T or H or D or J true. J true.

Clause 84 requires H or G or D true. None yet. We can set G true (영호 guilty). Good.

Clause 93 requires N or T or O or Y true. Y true.

Clause 107 requires N or J or I or M true. J true.

Clause 113 requires I or O or T true. I true.

Clause 114 requires Y or N or T or Z true. Y true.

Clause 115 requires R or D or O or S true. R true or D true. satisfied.
